title: Getting Started with React Router
author: Piyush
content: React Router lets you build single-page apps with URL-based navigation. Install react-router-dom, wrap your app in BrowserRouter, and define Routes with path and element props. Nested routes help share layout between pages.

title: localStorage Patterns in the Browser
author: Anita
content: Store JSON with JSON.stringify and parse on read. Use a stable key namespace. Remember storage events only fire across tabs, not same-tab updates—sync React state after writes.

title: Form Validation Without a Library
author: Piyush
content: Pure functions that return field errors keep validation testable. Run on submit and optionally on blur. Inline messages improve accessibility when paired with aria-invalid and aria-describedby.

title: Tailwind Utility-First Workflow
author: Marcus
content: Compose layouts with flex and grid utilities. Use focus-visible rings for keyboard users. Extract repeated patterns into components rather than copying long class strings everywhere.

title: Pagination State in Client Apps
author: Anita
content: Keep page index in component state when data is client-only. Slice the filtered array for the current page. Reset to page one when filters change so users never see empty pages accidentally.

title: Semantic HTML for Blog Posts
author: Sam
content: Use article for post bodies, time with datetime for timestamps, and heading levels that reflect outline order. Chips for tags can be ul lists with list-style-none for screen readers.

title: Why Vite for New React Projects
author: Marcus
content: Vite uses esbuild for dev transforms and Rollup for production bundles. Cold start and HMR stay fast as the project grows compared to bundler-dev-server approaches on large apps.

title: Managing CRUD UX Copy
author: Piyush
content: Confirm destructive actions. After create, navigate to detail or list with a clear success path. Empty states should explain how to add the first record and remain friendly when filters match nothing.

title: Comma-Separated Tags in Forms
author: Anita
content: Split on commas, trim whitespace, filter empty strings, and optionally lowercase for consistency. Display tags as chips on read with map. Store as array in your domain model.

title: Context API for Shared Client State
author: Sam
content: Provider at the root holds posts and mutators. Custom hooks encapsulate useContext and throw if used outside provider. Avoid putting every UI concern in context—keep it data-shaped.

title: ISO 8601 Timestamps in UIs
author: Marcus
content: Store UTC ISO strings. Format with Intl.DateTimeFormat or toLocaleString for locale-aware display. Show both created and updated when they differ so editors see recent changes.

title: Mini Admin Panels as Learning Projects
author: Piyush
content: Combine routing, forms, validation, and persistence without a server. Seed realistic data so search and filters feel meaningful. Stretch goals like mock REST teach fetch and async patterns next.
